content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_002_page_001.jpg

1. And I determined this for myself, not to come again to you in grief.

2. For if I grieve you, then who is the [one] making me glad except the [one] being grieved by me?

3. And I wrote this very [thing] to you, that having come I might not have grief from those from whom I ought to rejoice, being confident in all of you that my joy is [the joy] of all of you.

4. For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote to you through many tears, not that you might be grieved, but that you might know the love which I have more abundantly toward you.

5. And if anyone has caused grief, he has not grieved me, but in part — that I may not burden — all of you.

6. Sufficient for such a [one] is this punishment by the many,

7. so that on the contrary you should rather forgive and comfort, lest perhaps such a [one] be swallowed up by the excessive grief.

8. Therefore I urge you to confirm love toward him.

9. For to this [end] also I wrote, that I might know the proof of you, whether you are obedient in all [things].

10. And to whom you forgive anything, I also [forgive]. For what I also have forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, [it is] for your sake in [the] face of Christos,

11. that we might not be taken advantage of by the Satanas; for we are not ignorant of his schemes.

12. And having come to Troas for the good news of the Christos, and a door having been opened for me in [the] Lord,

13. I had no rest in the spirit of me, in not finding Titos the brother of me. But having taken leave of them, I went out into Makedonia.

14. But thanks [be] to God, the [one] always leading us in triumphal procession in the Christos and making manifest the fragrance of the knowledge of him through us in every place.

15. Because of Christos a sweet aroma we are to God among the [ones] being saved and among the [ones] perishing:

16. to the [ones] indeed an odor from death to death, and to the [others] an odor from life to life. And who is sufficient for these [things]?

17. For we are not as the many, peddling the word of God, but as from sincerity, but as from God, before God, in Christos we speak.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "through many tears":** δι᾽ ὀχλῶν δακρύων — Paul refers to a "tearful letter" (not 1 Corinthians) written in emotional anguish. This lost letter dealt with a specific offender.

2. **v.6 — "punishment by the many":** ἐπιτιμία ὑπὸ τῶν πλειόνων — the community's discipline was imposed by majority decision, revealing early church governance by congregational action.

3. **v.11 — "Satanas":** Σατανᾶς — the Hebrew adversary figure. Paul warns that excessive punishment serves Satan's purposes by destroying the offender rather than restoring him.

4. **v.14 — "triumphal procession":** θριαμβεύοντι — a Roman military term for the victor's parade. Strikingly, Paul places himself not as the triumphant general but as one led in the procession — a captive of Christ.

5. **v.15 — "sweet aroma":** εὐωδία — echoes the Old Testament sacrificial terminology (cf. Gen 8:21, Exod 29:18). Paul's ministry is a fragrant offering to God.

6. **v.17 — "peddling":** καπηλεύοντες — from κάπηλος, a retail dealer or huckster. Paul distances himself from those who market the gospel for profit.

— EMET, AI Translator
